title: Clean Fuels Weekly Commentary: LCFS Credit Prices Drop Along with Trading Activity WoW
content:
This Weekâ€™s Highlights

(24th â€“ 30th November 2025)
  • A total of 115,996 credits were exchanged as Type 1 trades, a fall of 51.61% week-over-week (WoW), and all non-zero trades witnessed a decrease of 49.50% WoW as 395,054 credits were traded in CA LCFS.
  • The weekly average CA LCFS credit price stood at $51.47/MT for Type 1 transfers, recording a 1.02% fall WoW; prices of all non-zero transfers saw a fall of 2.79%, priced at $54.45/MT.
  • The average LCFS spot credit price rose by 0.22% WoW; on 2nd December, the spot price stood at $51.13/MT, and the benchmark price stood at $51.00/MT.
  • D3 and D5 RIN prices remained unchanged WoW. D4 and D6 prices rose by 1.01% and 4.04% WoW

cCarbonâ€™s Market View

Last week, the U.S. Secretary of Agriculture, Brooke L. Rollins, announced that $7 million is available for advanced biofuel producers through the Advanced Biofuel Producer Payment Program under the One Big Beautiful Bill. The funding is intended to boost production of American-made biofuels, support energy independence, lower fuel prices, and create jobs.

CA LCFS Weekly Price and Volume Trend

[caption id="attachment_608381" align="alignnone" width="772"] Table 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average Type 1 transfer prices recorded a 1.02% WoW decrease, falling from $52.00 to $51.47 this week, and all non-zero transfer prices fell from $56.01 to $54.45, recording a fall of 2.79%. Type 1 trades witnessed 115,996 credits exchanged, recording a 51.61% fall WoW. The total transaction volumes for all non-zero trades saw a decrease of 49.50% WoW, reaching 395,054 credits. [caption id="attachment_608382" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption]

CA LCFS Daily Average Spot Price

[caption id="attachment_608383" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 2. California LCFS Weekly average spot prices and benchmark (*CC.Info OTC: Numbers are an average of broker prices reported to CC.info)[/caption] Last week, the average LCFS spot credit prices rose by 0.22% WoW, reaching $50.94/MT. On 2nd December, the spot price stood at $51.13/MT. The benchmark price, representing the future price of contracts expiring at the end of 2025, stood at $51.00/MT on 2nd December.

LCFS Weekly Trade Frequency and Volume

[caption id="attachment_608384" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 3. Trade frequency and volume, Source: California ARB[/caption] As per the data reported by CARB last week, 46 trades were reported in the market, with 4 high-volume trades. The highest transfer by price was $79.00 for a Type 2 transaction at a volume of 10,000 credits. The lowest transfer by price was $49.00 per credit for a Type 2 transaction reported for 11,952 credits. The largest volume of transfers involved 50,000 credits for a Type 2 transaction, priced at $63.50. The smallest volume of transfers involved 6 credits for a Type 1 transaction priced at $52.00.

CA LCFS Monthly Volumes and Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_608385" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 4. Total monthly volumes and associated prices, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average spot Type 1 price stood at $52.83 in November so far, falling from $53.80 in October. Trade volumes for the month of November stood at 3.72M as of 29th November, 2025.

CA LCFS Weekly Volume Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_608386" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 5. Volume Price Trend indicator for the CA LCFS market, Source: California ARB[/caption] The Volume Price Trend Indicator (VPT) helps identify the parity between supply and demand for a stock. The idea behind the indicator is to multiply the market volume of LCFS credits by the percentage change in its price. If the credit price declines, the value of the indicator falls. Across 24th â€“30th November 2025, total VPT fell by 0.98% WoW, with a fall in all non-zero credit prices for the last week.

Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS): RIN Weekly Price Trend

RINs are credits used for compliance and are the â€œcurrencyâ€ of the RFS program. Renewable fuel producers generate RINs, market participants trade RINs, and obligated parties obtain and then ultimately retire RINs for the compliance list. D3 and D5 RIN prices remained unchanged WoW. D4 and D6 prices rose by 1.01% and 4.04% WoW respectively. D3: Cellulosic biofuel D4: Biomass-based diesel D5: Advanced biofuels (produced from any type of renewable biomass except corn starch ethanol) D6: Conventional renewable biofuel ethanol derived from corn starch, or any other qualifying renewable fuel

Upcoming DatesÂ 

Key Clean Fuels Market Developments This Week

  • Secretary Rollins Announces Availability of $7 million for Advanced Biofuel Production: S. Secretary of Agriculture Brooke L. Rollins announced the U.S. Department of Agriculture is making $7 million in payments available to advanced biofuel producers nationwide through the Advanced Biofuel Producer Payment Program, which is a provision of the One Big Beautiful Bill. The funding is aimed at increasing the production of American grown biofuels. â€œPresident Trump is supporting and expanding American biofuel production through bold programs like this one, securing our Nationâ€™s energy dominance by investing in producers,â€ said Secretary Rollins. â€œThis home-grown biofuel production results in lower prices at the pumps, creates jobs, and puts Farmers First.â€
  • TRA Proposes New Countervailing Duties on USA HVO Biodiesel: The Trade Remedies Authority (TRA) has published its Statement of Essential Facts on imports of hydrotreated vegetable oil (HVO) biodiesel from the United States of America. The investigation, launched on 17 March 2025, covers HVO biodieselâ€”also known as renewable or green diesel. Following evidence that subsidised imports are causing material injury to UK biodiesel producers, the TRAâ€™s Statement of Essential Facts sets out its intended final recommendation for a fixed duty measure, with proposed countervailing duties of between Â£257.80 and Â£303.56 per tonne. Interested parties are invited to comment on the initial findings via the Trade Remedies Service by 19 December 2025.
